prompt: Tell me a bio of Jack Leach
evaluation_criteria: Factual accuracy of nationality (English), profession (cricketer), bowling style (left-arm orthodox), England Test debut details (2018, Christchurch), batting (left-handed), county cricket career. Verify exact debut date, specific squad selection details, and team membership claims.
output:
Jack Leach (full name: Matthew Jack Leach, born 22 June 1991 in Taunton, Somerset) is an English professional cricketer who plays as a left-arm orthodox spin bowler for England in Test cricket and for Somerset County Cricket Club domestically.

Key highlights of his career:
- Made his Test debut in March 2018 against New Zealand
- Became a cult hero during the 2019 Ashes, notably scoring the famous "1 not out" in the Headingley miracle alongside Ben Stokes
- Scored 92 as a nightwatchman against Ireland at Lord's in 2019
- Became England's first-choice spinner under Ben Stokes's captaincy
- Ended 2022 as the third-highest wicket-taker in world Test cricket
- Has 142 Test wickets at an average of 34.07 (as of April 2026)
- Has 551 first-class wickets for Somerset at an average of 27.76
- Has managed Crohn's disease since age 14 throughout his career
- Nicknames: "Leachy" and "The Nut"
